commands.review_summary:
The PR narrows Codex test atexit cleanup to UUID-named CodexBar stub executables and adds Swift Testing coverage for stub and real Codex command lines.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than launching live processes: current main uses `pgrep -f codex.*app-server`, which can match representative user Codex app-server commands. The PR's new test codifies the expected negative matches.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Owner-authored PR is protected from cleanup closure and has no narrow automated repair; maintainers can handle normal merge review.

target.title: fix: prevent tests from terminating user Codex processes
target.body:
## Summary
- restrict Codex test teardown to UUID-named executables created by the test suite
- stop broad process matching from targeting installed or user-owned Codex app-server processes
- add regression coverage for test stubs and representative real Codex command lines

## Risk
Test-only cleanup behavior. No application runtime changes.

## Proof
- `swift test --filter TestProcessCleanupTests`
- `make check`
- `swiftlint lint --strict Tests/CodexBarTests/TestProcessCleanupTests.swift`
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- existing user Codex app-server process remained alive after the focused test exited
target.author: steipete
